question: What is the full list of hook events a charm can observe? List them all.

claude_output.result:
Here is the complete list of all hook events a Juju charm can observe, organized by category:

---

## 1. Core Lifecycle Hooks

| `CharmBase.on` attribute | Shell hook name | When it fires |
|---|---|---|
| `install` | `install` | Once, on first deployment, before anything else |
| `config_changed` | `config-changed` | After install/upgrade/leader-elected, on restart, or whenever config changes |
| `start` | `start` | After the first `config-changed`; signal to start the workload |
| `stop` | `stop` | When the unit is being prepared for destruction |
| `remove` | `remove` | Final hook ever received; used for cleanup |
| `upgrade_charm` | `upgrade-charm` | After the charm directory is updated via `juju refresh` |
| `update_status` | `update-status` | Periodically (default every 5 min) for health reporting |

---

## 2. Leadership Hooks

| `CharmBase.on` attribute | Shell hook name | When it fires |
|---|---|---|
| `leader_elected` | `leader-elected` | On the unit that just became leader |
| `leader_settings_changed` | `leader-settings-changed` | On non-leader units when the leader writes to leadership settings *(deprecated since ops v2.4.0)* |

---

## 3. Relation Hooks *(one set per endpoint, named dynamically)*

Replace `<endpoint>` with the relation endpoint name from `metadata.yaml`.

| `CharmBase.on` attribute | Shell hook name | When it fires |
|---|---|---|
| `<endpoint>_relation_created` | `<endpoint>-relation-created` | When the relation is first established (before any remote units join) |
| `<endpoint>_relation_joined` | `<endpoint>-relation-joined` | When a remote unit joins the relation (once per remote unit) |
| `<endpoint>_relation_changed` | `<endpoint>-relation-changed` | After `relation-joined`, and whenever a remote unit updates its relation data |
| `<endpoint>_relation_departed` | `<endpoint>-relation-departed` | When a remote unit is leaving the relation |
| `<endpoint>_relation_broken` | `<endpoint>-relation-broken` | When the relation itself is being completely torn down |

---

## 4. Storage Hooks *(one set per storage, named dynamically)*

Replace `<storage>` with the storage name from `metadata.yaml`.

| `CharmBase.on` attribute | Shell hook name | When it fires |
|---|---|---|
| `<storage>_storage_attached` | `<storage>-storage-attached` | When a storage volume is provisioned and ready to use |
| `<storage>_storage_detaching` | `<storage>-storage-detaching` | Before a storage volume is removed |

---

## 5. Workload / Pebble Hooks *(Kubernetes sidecar charms only, named dynamically)*

Replace `<container>` with the container name from `metadata.yaml`.

| `CharmBase.on` attribute | Shell hook name | When it fires |
|---|---|---|
| `<container>_pebble_ready` | `<container>-pebble-ready` | When the Pebble daemon in the sidecar container becomes reachable |
| `<container>_pebble_check_failed` | `<container>-pebble-check-failed` | When a Pebble health check crosses its failure threshold |
| `<container>_pebble_check_recovered` | `<container>-pebble-check-recovered` | When a previously-failed Pebble health check passes again |
| `<container>_pebble_custom_notice` | `<container>-pebble-custom-notice` | When a custom Pebble notice is fired from within the container |

---

## 6. Secret Hooks

| `CharmBase.on` attribute | Shell hook name | Who receives it | When it fires |
|---|---|---|---|
| `secret_changed` | `secret-changed` | Observers/consumers | When the owner publishes a new revision of a tracked secret |
| `secret_rotate` | `secret-rotate` | Owner | When the secret's rotation policy interval elapses |
| `secret_expired` | `secret-expired` | Owner | When a secret revision's expiry time is reached |
| `secret_remove` | `secret-remove` | Owner | When a revision is no longer tracked by any consumer and can be deleted |

---

## 7. Status Collection Hooks *(ops framework-level, not shell hooks)*

These fire at the end of every hook execution to let the charm report status in a composable way via `event.add_status(...)`.

| `CharmBase.on` attribute | Who receives it | When it fires |
|---|---|---|
| `collect_app_status` | Leader unit only | End of every hook, to set **application** status |
| `collect_unit_status` | All units | End of every hook, to set **unit** status |

---

## 8. Action Hooks *(one per action, named dynamically)*

Replace `<action>` with the action name from `actions.yaml`.

| `CharmBase.on` attribute | When it fires |
|---|---|
| `<action>_action` | When an operator runs `juju run <unit> <action>` |

---

## 9. Series Upgrade Hooks *(machine charms only; deprecated, removed in Juju 4.0)*

| `CharmBase.on` attribute | Shell hook name | When it fires |
|---|---|---|
| `pre_series_upgrade` | `pre-series-upgrade` | Before an OS series upgrade begins |
| `post_series_upgrade` | `post-series-upgrade` | After an OS series upgrade completes |

---

## 10. Metrics Hook *(deprecated, removed in Juju 3.6.11 / 4.0)*

| `CharmBase.on` attribute | Shell hook name | When it fires |
|---|---|---|
| `collect_metrics` | `collect-metrics` | Every 5 minutes, to collect custom metrics (use `prometheus_scrape` library instead) |

---

### Quick Count Summary

| Category | Events |
|---|---|
| Core Lifecycle | 7 |
| Leadership | 2 |
| Relation | **5 × N endpoints** |
| Storage | **2 × N storages** |
| Pebble/Workload (K8s) | **4 × N containers** |
| Secrets | 4 |
| Status Collection | 2 |
| Actions | **1 × N actions** |
| Series Upgrade (deprecated) | 2 |
| Metrics (deprecated) | 1 |
